summary: Large decomp/matching PR for fighter common file ftCo_0A01. It added implementations and partial-progress source for many ftCo_0A01 functions, updated header prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures, added subsystem includes for camera, stage/ground, vector, and collision helpers, and produced 11 newly matched functions plus 25 unmatched-item improvements per the bot report. Evidence is mixed because the diff is truncated and there are no human review comments or PR body.

key_files.role: Main decompilation source; added thousands of lines replacing placeholders and generated-looking gotos with C implementations for ftCo_0A01 functions, stage-specific logic, collision checks, island/item iteration, and helper wrappers.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: added includes cm/camera.h, gr/grcastle.h, gr/grgreatbay.h, gr/grrcruise.h, lb/lbvector.h
diff_excerpt: /// #ftCo_800A229C replaced with s32 ftCo_800A229C(Fighter* fp, Vec3* arg1)
diff_excerpt: /// #ftCo_800A2C80 replaced with s32 ftCo_800A2C80(Fighter* fp)
diff_excerpt: /// #ftCo_800A3908 replaced with bool ftCo_800A3908(Fighter* fp, bool arg1)
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists 11 new matches and 25 improvements in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01

key_files.role: Header/prototype cleanup; changed several static declarations from unknown return/params to typed signatures.
evidence_refs: raw diff lines 6182-6197: ftCo_800A229C bool->s32, ftCo_800AEA8C UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS->void Fighter*, ftCo_800AEFB8 UNK_RET->void, ftCo_800A2C80 UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS->s32 Fighter*
raw diff lines 6203-6218: ftCo_800A4768, ftCo_800A6A98, ftCo_800A6D2C, ftCo_800A6FC4, ftCo_800A8210, ftCo_800A866C prototypes typed

what_changed: Added typed implementations in ftCo_0A01.c for previously placeholder-marked functions, including ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, and ftCo_800A3908 as visible in the excerpt.

Added includes for camera, castle, great bay, rainbow cruise, and vector APIs, consistent with newly introduced calls such as Camera_8003118C, grCastle_801CDF54, grGreatBay_801F66A4, grLib_801C9E60, and lbVector_Normalize.
Converted inlineL0 from a goto-heavy generated shape into a for loop over HSD_GObj_Entities->items plus a switch on stage_info.internal_stage_id.
Simplified ftCo_800A2718 to a null check followed by inlineL0(arg0) || inlineL1(arg0), while also adding chained dontinline wrappers for ftCo_800A2718.
Added noinline/dontinline wrapper helpers around functions such as ftCo_800A1B38, ftCo_800A1C44, ftCo_800A1F3C, ftCo_800A2718, and ftCo_IsGrabbing.
Updated ftCo_0A01.h declarations to replace multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete return types and Fighter*/Vec3* parameters.
Bot report recorded 11 new 100% matches and 25 improvements, but also one unmatched-item regression for ftCo_800A2718 from 82.34% to 77.32%.

smart_moves.text: Recovered header signatures alongside body work instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS after the source exposed argument and return types.
Replaced a generated-looking item traversal with a normal for loop over HSD_GObj_Entities->items, preserving the item pointer and early-continue checks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: inlineL0 changed from labels loop_8/block_3/block_7 to for (cur = HSD_GObj_Entities->items; cur != NULL; cur = cur->next)

smart_moves.text: Used switch dispatch for stage-specific behavior instead of preserving a long branch ladder in inlineL0 and ftCo_800A229C.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: inlineL0 switch(stage_info.internal_stage_id) with STORY, ZEBES, ONETT, default
diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A229C switch(stage) with RCRUISE, BIGBLUE, ICEMTN, default

decomp_lessons.lesson: When body recovery proves a function's type, update the owning header declarations in the same PR rather than retaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Generated label/goto traversal over object lists can often be expressed as a natural for loop with continue guards, improving reviewability while preserving control flow.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: inlineL0 old labels loop_8/block_3/block_7 removed
diff_excerpt: inlineL0 new for loop over HSD_GObj_Entities->items

decomp_lessons.lesson: Stage-id branch ladders in decompiled code should be checked for switch-form reconstruction when cases are discrete stage constants.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: inlineL0 new switch on stage_info.internal_stage_id
diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A229C new switch(stage) over RCRUISE, BIGBLUE, ICEMTN

naming_conventions.text: Address-style function names were retained for unrecovered semantics, e.g. ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800A3908, while local variable names were role/shape based such as fp, data, floor_pos, floor_normal, line_id, flags, stage, margin, bottom, island.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: function declarations and local variables in ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800A3908

naming_conventions.text: Temporary internal wrappers use explicit suffixes such as _noinline, _noinline2, and _dontinline, making codegen-steering helpers visible rather than giving them semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A1B38_noinline, ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline, ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline2, ftCo_800A2718_dontinline2, ftCo_800A2718_dontinline3

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added noinline/dontinline wrapper functions around existing helpers, apparently to steer call/inlining shape for matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline and ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline2 chain calls
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The PR evidence shows wrappers and bot score improvements but does not include narrow objdiff rationale for each wrapper; treat as matching tactic needing evidence before generalizing.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used C switch forms for stage-specific branch structure in ftCo_800A229C and inlineL0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Supported by visible diff shape and global standard favoring canonical switch forms, but exact match status of these functions is partial in bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced typed calls into stage, camera, map, vector, and collision helpers instead of raw pointer math in the visible excerpts.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Camera_8003118C, Stage_GetBlastZone*Offset, Ground_801C4368, mpCheckFloor, mpCheckLeftWall, mpCheckRightWall, lbVector_Normalize
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The excerpt supports API usage, but the full 198KB diff is truncated, so this should not be generalized to the entire PR.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Tracked matching progress via bot checkdiff-style report showing 11 new matches, 25 improvements, and one regression.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: 25 improvements in unmatched items
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Bot report is strong evidence for score movement, but not a substitute for per-symbol author rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update header prototypes when body recovery proves signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2524 updated ftCo_0A01.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete return and parameter types while adding bodies in ftCo_0A01.c.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural loop reconstruction for generated object-list traversal
accepted_candidate_records.text: inlineL0 was converted from label/goto traversal of HSD_GObj_Entities->items to a for loop with continue guards and a final stage switch.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Noinline/dontinline wrappers require per-use evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2524 added several explicit wrapper helpers such as ftCo_800A1B38_noinline and ftCo_800A2718_dontinline3; keep this as a tactic candidate, not a style rule, because the slice lacks per-wrapper objdiff rationale.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the noinline/dontinline wrapper pattern as an accepted global convention from this PR alone; the evidence shows usage but not necessity for each wrapper.
Do not infer semantic names for address-style ftCo symbols from this PR; the diff keeps address-style names and the PR body is empty.
Do not claim every changed function matched; the bot report distinguishes 11 new matches, 25 partial improvements, and one regression.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are present.
